checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: By 2017-12-31 AR fell back to 424,679,000 while revenue rose, indicating the 2016 divergence was period-specific, not a persistent multi-period trend

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill grew from 262,376,000 (2016-12-31) to 814,309,000 (2024-12-31) and IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill from 11,844,000 (2016) to 267,357,000 (2024), but step-changes coincide with acquisition dates (e.g., goodwill 319,272,000 at 2017-12-31, 483,584,000 at 2018-12-31)
Total Assets grew commensurately from 1,360,259,000 (2016-12-31) to 2,868,343,000 (2024-12-31); soft-asset growth is roughly proportionate to acquisition-driven total-asset growth

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss 167,528,000 (FY2023) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities 422,158,000 (FY2023) — OCF well above NI
NetIncomeLoss 103,993,000 (FY2024) vs OCF 194,419,000 (FY2024); OCF consistently exceeds NI across FY2018-FY2024, indicating no adverse accrual buildup

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No inventory line item is reported (service/logistics company). GrossProfit 425,437,000 on revenue 3,495,644,000 (FY2020) vs GrossProfit 889,505,000 on revenue 5,340,490,000 (FY2022) — margin expansion tracks the freight-rate cycle rather than diverging from revenue

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss swung 356,948,000 (FY2022) -> 167,528,000 (FY2023) -> 103,993,000 (FY2024); earnings are highly volatile and clearly cyclical, the opposite of artificial smoothing

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent fell from 7,000,000 (2014-12-31) to 5,200,000 (2015-12-31) and remained only 5,400,000 (2016-12-31)
Over the same window AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 401,803,000 (2014) to 473,608,000 (2016), so allowance fell from ~1.74% to ~1.14% of receivables while receivables expanded

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: A 10-K/A and multiple 10-Q/A filings are all dated 2018-01-05 (restatement cluster), plus an earlier 10-Q/A on 2016-10-31

Two parallel revenue tags exist for FY2016 — SalesRevenueServicesNet 3,572,790,000 and RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax 2,750,449,000 — consistent with a material revenue-presentation restatement
SEC correspondence (UPLOAD/CORRESP) appears around 2017-12/2018-01 and again 2022-07 through 2022-09, and an NT 10-Q was filed 2002-08-15

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Point-in-time data spans 2007-2025 across revenue, receivables, allowance, goodwill, intangibles, cash flow and equity, sufficient for multi-period trend analysis
Some series end early (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent stops at 2019-12-31; GrossProfit stops at FY2022), limiting the very latest receivables/margin tests but not the overall assessment

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Revenue presentation change (gross-vs-net / principal-vs-agent), evidenced by the 2018-01-05 10-K/A and 10-Q/A cluster and two co-existing revenue tags. FY2016 gross revenue of 3,572,790,000 was restated to 2,750,449,000 under the new revenue framework, an ~822M reduction, with an offsetting reduction to cost of services. This is a presentation/classification matter that inflates reported top-line (and cost) without necessarily changing net income.

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K/A and multiple 10-Q/A all filed 2018-01-05, indicating restatement of previously issued statements

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Bad-debt allowance appears under-provisioned in 2015-2016: the allowance fell to 5.2-5.4M even as gross receivables rose to 473.6M, lowering the reserve ratio from ~1.74% to ~1.14%. If under-reserved, bad-debt expense is understated and net receivables/net income overstated. Notably the company later sharply increased the allowance (to 20.1M in 2021 and 38.6M in 2022), consistent with prior periods having been light.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=5,200,000 (2015-12-31) and 5,400,000 (2016-12-31) versus 7,000,000 (2014-12-31), while AR grew to 473,608,000 (2016-12-31)
Allowance later rose to 20,100,000 (2021-12-31) and 38,600,000 (2022-12-31), suggesting earlier reserves were comparatively low

top_signals: Confirmed restatement cluster (10-K/A plus multiple 10-Q/A filed 2018-01-05; earlier 10-Q/A 2016-10-31)
Revenue-presentation change: FY2016 revenue restated from 3,572,790,000 gross to 2,750,449,000 net
2016 receivables +24.6% vs revenue +1.3%, with allowance ratio simultaneously falling to ~1.14%
Mitigants: OCF consistently exceeds NI and earnings are cyclically volatile (no smoothing), reducing fraud likelihood; soft-asset growth is acquisition-driven and proportionate
